title: sideritis peyrei subsp. pastoris
intro: Sideritis peyrei subsp. pastoris, also known as Pastor's Ironwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's health benefits are thought to include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and even exhibit neuroprotective properties. In traditional herbal preparations, Pastor's Ironwort is often used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although its use and effectiveness have not been extensively scientifically studied.
